code: @Manual{,   title = {readoecd: Access OECD Data},   author = {Charles Coverdale},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.3.0},   url = {https://github.com/charlescoverdale/readoecd}, }

what: Download and Tidy Data from the OECD
title: Download and Tidy Data from the OECD
text: readoecd provides clean, tidy access OECD economic data directly R.

title: Download and Tidy Data from the OECD
text: existing R package called OECD, written Eric Persson first published CRAN 2016. generic API wrapper - rather providing named functions specific datasets, exposes raw OECD SDMX API directly. typical workflow requires user first browse available datasets search_dataset(), retrieve dimension codes get_data_structure(), construct filter string manually finally calling get_dataset(). gives maximum flexibility places burden knowing OECD’s dataset catalogue dimension codes entirely user. CRAN version (v0.2.5, released December 2021) currently broken. July 2024, OECD migrated API old endpoint (stats.oecd.org) new SDMX 3.0 REST API (sdmx.oecd.org). CRAN version still points old endpoint, longer returns data. Despite broken, package still attracts around 700–1,400 downloads per month - reflecting many analysts depend OECD data R working alternative. Eric’s GitHub version partially updated (expersso/OECD). updated package August 2025 target new API, version submitted CRAN widely known. remains generic wrapper: still requires users know dataset IDs construct filter strings. readoecd takes different approach. Rather wrapping raw API, provides curated named functions commonly needed OECD datasets. Users need know anything SDMX, dataset identifiers, dimension codes - get_oecd_gdp(c("AUS", "GBR"), start_year = 2010) just works. Output columns consistent across functions, data cached locally repeated calls instant.

title: Download and Tidy Data from the OECD
code: install.packages("readoecd")  # Or install the development version from GitHub # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("charlescoverdale/readoecd")

what: Looking for a specific dataset or variable treatment?
title: Download and Tidy Data from the OECD
text: function readoecd returns single, opinionated series - example, get_oecd_cpi() returns annual headline inflation rate, get_oecd_health() returns total health expenditure share GDP. need different cut data, best starting point OECD Data Explorer, lets browse filter OECD dataset interactively. Examples things might want go beyond readoecd currently provides: CPI expenditure category (food, housing, transport) rather headline total Health expenditure financing source (government-vs --pocket) rather -sources aggregate GDP constant prices (volume, real growth) rather current prices Tax revenue tax type (income tax, VAT, corporate tax) rather total Unemployment age group sex rather -persons total Trade goods services separately rather combined current account

title: Download and Tidy Data from the OECD
code: library(readoecd)

title: Download and Tidy Data from the OECD
text: Source: OECD National Accounts (Main Aggregates, Table 1). Annual GDP measured using expenditure approach current prices, millions US dollars exchange rates. Coverage broad - OECD members data 1990s, earlier. GDP USD PPP used cross-country comparisons available; package falls back exchange-rate USD PPP published given country-year.
code: # Largest OECD economies in the most recent year gdp <- get_oecd_gdp("all", start_year = 2015) latest <- gdp[gdp$year == max(gdp$year), ] head(latest[order(-latest$value), c("country_name", "year", "value")], 5) #>        country_name year    value #>   United States     2023 27357916 #>         Germany     2023  4457178 #>           Japan     2023  4212945 #>  United Kingdom     2023  3081866 #>          France     2023  2923489  # G7 comparison since 2000 g7 <- get_oecd_gdp(c("CAN", "FRA", "DEU", "ITA", "JPN", "GBR", "USA"),                    start_year = 2000)

title: Download and Tidy Data from the OECD
text: Source: OECD Prices database (COICOP 1999 classification). Annual year--year percentage change Consumer Price Index total household expenditure, seasonally adjusted. standard harmonised measure headline inflation used cross-country comparisons. Coverage varies country generally available 1970s–1980s onwards.
code: # Inflation through the post-COVID price surge cpi <- get_oecd_cpi(c("AUS", "GBR", "USA", "DEU"), start_year = 2018) cpi[cpi$year == 2022, c("country_name", "year", "value")] #>    country_name year value #>       Australia 2022   6.6 #>         Germany 2022   8.7 #>  United Kingdom 2022   9.1 #>   United States 2022   8.0

title: Download and Tidy Data from the OECD
text: Source: OECD Labour Force Statistics (Harmonised Unemployment Rates). Monthly seasonally-adjusted unemployment rate percentage labour force, persons aged 15 . harmonised series adjusts differences national survey methodologies, making standard series cross-country comparisons. Coverage varies country generally starts 1980s–1990s.
code: # Track unemployment through the COVID shock unemp <- get_oecd_unemployment(c("AUS", "GBR", "USA"), start_year = 2018)  # Peak unemployment by country during 2020 peak_2020 <- unemp[substr(unemp$period, 1, 4) == "2020", ] aggregate(value ~ country_name, data = peak_2020, FUN = max) #>    country_name value #>       Australia  7.47 #>  United Kingdom  5.10 #>   United States 14.70

title: Download and Tidy Data from the OECD
text: Source: OECD Revenue Statistics Comparator. Total tax revenue percentage GDP, covering levels government (central, state, local) tax types (income, payroll, property, goods services, taxes). standard measure overall tax burden used cross-country fiscal analysis. Annual data; countries covered early 1990s.
code: # Tax burden: Nordics vs Anglo-Saxon economies tax <- get_oecd_tax(c("DNK", "SWE", "NOR", "AUS", "GBR", "USA"),                     start_year = 2010)  latest_tax <- tax[tax$year == max(tax$year), c("country_name", "year", "value")] latest_tax[order(-latest_tax$value), ] #>    country_name year value #>         Denmark 2022  46.0 #>          Sweden 2022  42.6 #>          Norway 2022  42.2 #>  United Kingdom 2022  35.3 #>       Australia 2022  29.5 #>   United States 2022  27.7

title: Download and Tidy Data from the OECD
text: Source: OECD National Accounts (General Government Accounts, NAAG). General government net lending/borrowing percentage GDP, consistent System National Accounts (SNA) definition. positive value indicates surplus; negative value indicates deficit. Annual data; OECD members covered 1990s.
code: # Fiscal positions during and after the pandemic deficit <- get_oecd_deficit(c("AUS", "GBR", "USA", "DEU"), start_year = 2018) deficit[deficit$year %in% 2019:2022, c("country_name", "year", "value")] #>    country_name year  value #>       Australia 2019  0.02 #>       Australia 2020 -4.45 #>       Australia 2021 -4.97 #>       Australia 2022 -1.70

title: Download and Tidy Data from the OECD
text: Source: System Health Accounts (SHA). Total current health expenditure percentage GDP, covering financing sources - government schemes, compulsory health insurance, voluntary health insurance, --pocket payments. Based internationally standardised SHA 2011 framework developed jointly OECD, , Eurostat. Annual data; coverage typically 2000 onwards.
code: # Which OECD country spends the most on health? health <- get_oecd_health("all", start_year = 2015) latest_hlth <- health[health$year == max(health$year), ] head(latest_hlth[order(-latest_hlth$value), c("country_name", "year", "value")], 5) #>    country_name year value #>   United States 2022  16.6 #>         Germany 2022  12.7 #>          France 2022  11.9 #>     Switzerland 2022  11.3 #>       Australia 2022   9.7

title: Download and Tidy Data from the OECD
text: Source: Education Glance (EAG) - UOE Finance indicators. Total expenditure educational institutions percentage GDP, aggregated across levels education (ISCED 0–8, early childhood tertiary). Covers public private expenditure. Published annually; data typically available two-year lag, covering OECD members mid-2000s.
code: # Education spending trends across selected countries edu <- get_oecd_education(c("AUS", "GBR", "USA", "KOR", "FIN"),                           start_year = 2005)  latest_edu <- edu[edu$year == max(edu$year), c("country_name", "year", "value")] latest_edu[order(-latest_edu$value), ]

title: Download and Tidy Data from the OECD
text: Source: OECD Income Distribution Database (IDD). Gini coefficient disposable household income (taxes transfers), scale 0 (perfect equality) 1 (maximum inequality). Follows OECD METH2012 methodology comparability across countries time. Annual data; coverage varies country, typically mid-1990s.
code: # Income inequality: how do OECD countries compare? gini <- get_oecd_inequality("all", start_year = 2010) latest_gini <- gini[gini$year == max(gini$year), ]  # Most and least equal OECD economies head(latest_gini[order(latest_gini$value), c("country_name", "year", "value")], 5) head(latest_gini[order(-latest_gini$value), c("country_name", "year", "value")], 5)

title: Download and Tidy Data from the OECD
text: Source: OECD Productivity Database (PDB). GDP per hour worked USD PPP available, falling back GDP per capita. GDP per hour worked standard measure labour productivity used cross-country comparisons, adjusts differences average working hours across countries. Annual data; coverage varies country.
code: # Productivity rankings across all OECD members prod <- get_oecd_productivity("all", start_year = 2010) latest_prod <- prod[prod$year == max(prod$year), ] head(latest_prod[order(-latest_prod$value), c("country_name", "year", "value")], 5)

title: Download and Tidy Data from the OECD
text: Source: OECD Balance Payments Statistics. Annual current account balance rest world, millions US dollars current exchange rates. positive value indicates surplus (exports exceeding imports goods, services, income, transfers); negative value indicates deficit. Coverage annual; countries available 1990s.
code: # Identify persistent surplus and deficit countries ca <- get_oecd_current_account("all", start_year = 2010) latest_ca <- ca[ca$year == max(ca$year), ]  # Largest surpluses head(latest_ca[order(-latest_ca$value), c("country_name", "year", "value")], 5)  # Largest deficits head(latest_ca[order(latest_ca$value), c("country_name", "year", "value")], 5)

title: Download and Tidy Data from the OECD
code: # See all available country codes list_oecd_countries()  # Clear the local cache to force fresh downloads clear_oecd_cache()

title: Download and Tidy Data from the OECD
text: get_oecd_*() functions return tidy long-format data frame consistent columns:

title: Download and Tidy Data from the OECD
text: Downloaded data stored user cache directory (tools::R_user_dir("readoecd", "cache")). Subsequent calls return cached copy instantly. Use refresh = TRUE force re-download, clear_oecd_cache() wipe cached files.

title: Download and Tidy Data from the OECD
text: readoecd uses OECD Data Explorer REST API (sdmx.oecd.org). OECD periodically migrates dataset identifiers - function stops working, check package update file issue.

title: Download and Tidy Data from the OECD
text: readoecd package part suite R packages economic financial data:

text: OECD, SDMX, international economic data, GDP, unemployment, trade, education, health, environment, OECD Data Explorer, open data, cross-country comparison, R package

title: Download and Tidy Data from the OECD

title: Check OECD API connectivity — check_oecd_api
text: Tests OECD API making small live request. Useful diagnosing connectivity issues confirming API responding normally.

title: Check OECD API connectivity — check_oecd_api
code: check_oecd_api()

title: Check OECD API connectivity — check_oecd_api
text: Invisibly returns TRUE API reachable, otherwise throws error.

title: Check OECD API connectivity — check_oecd_api
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ check_oecd_api() } # }

what: Clear the readoecd local cache — clear_oecd_cache
title: Clear the readoecd local cache — clear_oecd_cache
text: Deletes files cached readoecd local machine. clearing, next call get_oecd_*() function re-download OECD API.

title: Clear the readoecd local cache — clear_oecd_cache
code: clear_oecd_cache()

title: Clear the readoecd local cache — clear_oecd_cache
text: Invisibly returns number files deleted.

title: Clear the readoecd local cache — clear_oecd_cache
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(readoecd.cache_dir = tempdir()) clear_oecd_cache() #> Warning: cannot remove file '/tmp/Rtmp3F4o0B/bslib-246362e7e3ff6191071d5f9b40ba8d62', reason 'Directory not empty' #> Warning: cannot remove file '/tmp/Rtmp3F4o0B/downlit', reason 'Directory not empty' #> Cleared 2 cached files. options(op) # }

what: Get OECD CPI inflation data — get_oecd_cpi
title: Get OECD CPI inflation data — get_oecd_cpi
text: Downloads (caches) annual consumer price inflation OECD member countries OECD Prices database (COICOP 1999 classification).

title: Get OECD CPI inflation data — get_oecd_cpi
code: get_oecd_cpi(countries = "all", start_year = 1990, refresh = FALSE)

title: Get OECD CPI inflation data — get_oecd_cpi
text: countries Character vector ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes, "" available OECD members. Defaults "". Run list_oecd_countries() see available codes. start_year Numeric. Earliest year include. Defaults 1990. refresh Logical. TRUE, re-download even cached copy exists. Defaults FALSE.

title: Get OECD CPI inflation data — get_oecd_cpi
text: data frame columns: country ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code (character) country_name English country name (character) year Calendar year (integer) series "CPI_INFLATION" (character) value Annual CPI inflation rate (numeric) unit "% change, year--year" (character)

title: Get OECD CPI inflation data — get_oecd_cpi
text: Returns year--year percentage change Consumer Price Index (CPI) total expenditure, seasonally adjusted. standard harmonised measure headline inflation used cross-country comparisons.

title: Get OECD CPI inflation data — get_oecd_cpi
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(readoecd.cache_dir = tempdir()) cpi <- try(get_oecd_cpi(c("AUS", "GBR", "USA"), start_year = 2000)) #> Downloading from OECD API... if (!inherits(cpi, "try-error")) head(cpi) #>    country country_name year        series    value                   unit #> 77     AUS    Australia 2000 CPI_INFLATION 4.457435 % change, year-on-year #> 76     AUS    Australia 2001 CPI_INFLATION 4.407135 % change, year-on-year #> 75     AUS    Australia 2002 CPI_INFLATION 2.981575 % change, year-on-year #> 74     AUS    Australia 2003 CPI_INFLATION 2.732596 % change, year-on-year #> 73     AUS    Australia 2004 CPI_INFLATION 2.343255 % change, year-on-year #> 72     AUS    Australia 2005 CPI_INFLATION 2.691832 % change, year-on-year options(op) # }

what: Get OECD trade (balance of payments) data — get_oecd_current_account
title: Get OECD trade (balance of payments) data — get_oecd_current_account
text: Downloads (caches) annual current account balance data OECD member countries OECD Balance Payments (BOP) database.

title: Get OECD trade (balance of payments) data — get_oecd_current_account
code: get_oecd_current_account(countries = "all", start_year = 1990, refresh = FALSE)

title: Get OECD trade (balance of payments) data — get_oecd_current_account
text: countries Character vector ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes, "" available OECD members. Defaults "". Run list_oecd_countries() see available codes. start_year Numeric. Earliest year include. Defaults 1990. refresh Logical. TRUE, re-download even cached copy exists. Defaults FALSE.

title: Get OECD trade (balance of payments) data — get_oecd_current_account
text: data frame columns: country ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code (character) country_name English country name (character) year Calendar year (integer) series "CURRENT_ACCOUNT" "CURRENT_ACCOUNT_GOODS_SERVICES" (character) value Current account balance millions USD (numeric) unit "Millions USD (exchange rate)" (character)

title: Get OECD trade (balance of payments) data — get_oecd_current_account
text: Returns current account balance (credits minus debits) rest world, millions US dollars current exchange rates. positive value indicates current account surplus; negative value indicates deficit.

title: Get OECD trade (balance of payments) data — get_oecd_current_account
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(readoecd.cache_dir = tempdir()) trade <- try(get_oecd_current_account(c("AUS", "DEU", "USA"), start_year = 2000)) #> Downloading from OECD API... #> Error in value[[3L]](cond) : Failed to reach the OECD API. #> ℹ Check your internet connection and try again. #> ℹ If the problem persists, the OECD may have changed their API. #> ℹ Check for a package update or report at #>   <https://github.com/charlescoverdale/readoecd/issues> if (!inherits(trade, "try-error")) head(trade) options(op) # }

what: Get OECD government deficit data — get_oecd_deficit
title: Get OECD government deficit data — get_oecd_deficit
text: Downloads (caches) general government net lending/borrowing share GDP OECD member countries OECD National Accounts (NAAG) database.

title: Get OECD government deficit data — get_oecd_deficit
code: get_oecd_deficit(countries = "all", start_year = 1990, refresh = FALSE)

title: Get OECD government deficit data — get_oecd_deficit
text: countries Character vector ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes, "" available OECD members. Defaults "". Run list_oecd_countries() see available codes. start_year Numeric. Earliest year include. Defaults 1990. refresh Logical. TRUE, re-download even cached copy exists. Defaults FALSE.

title: Get OECD government deficit data — get_oecd_deficit
text: data frame columns: country ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code (character) country_name English country name (character) year Calendar year (integer) series "GOVT_NET_LENDING" (character) value Net lending/borrowing share GDP (numeric). Positive = surplus; negative = deficit. unit "% GDP" (character)

title: Get OECD government deficit data — get_oecd_deficit
text: Net lending/borrowing difference government revenue expenditure, expressed percentage GDP. positive value indicates surplus (government saving); negative value indicates deficit (government borrowing). standard fiscal balance measure used cross-country comparisons consistent System National Accounts (SNA) definition.

title: Get OECD government deficit data — get_oecd_deficit
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(readoecd.cache_dir = tempdir()) deficit <- try(get_oecd_deficit(c("AUS", "GBR", "USA"), start_year = 2000)) #> Downloading from OECD API... #> Error in value[[3L]](cond) : Failed to reach the OECD API. #> ℹ Check your internet connection and try again. #> ℹ If the problem persists, the OECD may have changed their API. #> ℹ Check for a package update or report at #>   <https://github.com/charlescoverdale/readoecd/issues> if (!inherits(deficit, "try-error")) head(deficit) options(op) # }

title: Get OECD GDP data — get_oecd_gdp
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(readoecd.cache_dir = tempdir()) gdp <- try(get_oecd_gdp(c("AUS", "GBR", "USA"), start_year = 2010)) #> Downloading from OECD API...  if (!inherits(gdp, "try-error")) {   # Largest OECD economies   latest <- gdp[gdp$year == max(gdp$year), ]   head(latest[order(-latest$value), c("country_name", "value")], 10) } #>     country_name   value #> 2 United Kingdom 4538475 options(op) # }

title: Get OECD income inequality data — get_oecd_inequality

title: Get OECD productivity data — get_oecd_productivity
text: Returns GDP per hour worked available (USD purchasing power parities), falling back GDP per capita. Values current prices.

title: Get OECD productivity data — get_oecd_productivity
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(readoecd.cache_dir = tempdir()) prod <- try(get_oecd_productivity(c("AUS", "GBR", "USA"), start_year = 2000)) #> Downloading from OECD API... if (!inherits(prod, "try-error")) head(prod) #>     country country_name year       series    value      unit #> 73      AUS    Australia 2000 GDP_PER_HOUR 34.09458 USD_PPP_H #> 74      AUS    Australia 2000 GDP_PER_HOUR 53.89475 USD_PPP_H #> 75      AUS    Australia 2000 GDP_PER_HOUR 44.73481 USD_PPP_H #> 220     AUS    Australia 2000 GDP_PER_HOUR 77.13215 USD_PPP_H #> 76      AUS    Australia 2001 GDP_PER_HOUR 47.77077 USD_PPP_H #> 77      AUS    Australia 2001 GDP_PER_HOUR 55.96281 USD_PPP_H options(op) # }

what: Get OECD tax revenue data — get_oecd_tax
title: Get OECD tax revenue data — get_oecd_tax
text: Downloads (caches) total tax revenue share GDP OECD member countries OECD Revenue Statistics Comparator database.

title: Get OECD tax revenue data — get_oecd_tax
code: get_oecd_tax(countries = "all", start_year = 1990, refresh = FALSE)

title: Get OECD tax revenue data — get_oecd_tax
text: countries Character vector ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes, "" available OECD members. Defaults "". Run list_oecd_countries() see available codes. start_year Numeric. Earliest year include. Defaults 1990. refresh Logical. TRUE, re-download even cached copy exists. Defaults FALSE.

title: Get OECD tax revenue data — get_oecd_tax
text: data frame columns: country ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code (character) country_name English country name (character) year Calendar year (integer) series "TAX_REVENUE" (character) value Total tax revenue share GDP (numeric) unit "% GDP" (character)

title: Get OECD tax revenue data — get_oecd_tax
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(readoecd.cache_dir = tempdir()) tax <- try(get_oecd_tax(c("AUS", "GBR", "USA"), start_year = 2000)) #> Downloading from OECD API... #> Error in value[[3L]](cond) : Failed to reach the OECD API. #> ℹ Check your internet connection and try again. #> ℹ If the problem persists, the OECD may have changed their API. #> ℹ Check for a package update or report at #>   <https://github.com/charlescoverdale/readoecd/issues> if (!inherits(tax, "try-error")) head(tax) options(op) # }

title: Get OECD unemployment rates — get_oecd_unemployment
text: Downloads (caches) monthly harmonised unemployment rates OECD member countries OECD Labour Force Statistics database.

title: Get OECD unemployment rates — get_oecd_unemployment
code: get_oecd_unemployment(countries = "all", start_year = 2000, refresh = FALSE)

title: Get OECD unemployment rates — get_oecd_unemployment
text: countries Character vector ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes, "" 38 OECD members. Defaults "". Run list_oecd_countries() see available codes. start_year Numeric. Earliest year include. Defaults 2000. refresh Logical. TRUE, re-download even cached copy exists. Defaults FALSE.

title: Get OECD unemployment rates — get_oecd_unemployment
text: data frame columns: country ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code (character) country_name English country name (character) period Calendar month "YYYY-MM" format (character) series "Unemployment rate" (character) value Unemployment rate percentage labour force (numeric) unit "% labour force" (character)

title: Get OECD unemployment rates — get_oecd_unemployment
text: Returns seasonally adjusted unemployment rate percentage labour force, persons aged 15 (total, sexes). standard harmonised series used cross-country comparisons.

title: Get OECD unemployment rates — get_oecd_unemployment
code: # \donttest{ op <- options(readoecd.cache_dir = tempdir()) # All OECD members since 2010 une <- try(get_oecd_unemployment(start_year = 2010)) #> Downloading from OECD API...  # Australia and UK since 2020 une <- try(get_oecd_unemployment(c("AUS", "GBR"), start_year = 2020)) #> Downloading from OECD API...  if (!inherits(une, "try-error")) {   # Which country had the highest unemployment in 2020?   une2020 <- une[startsWith(une$period, "2020"), ]   une2020_avg <- aggregate(value ~ country_name, une2020, mean)   head(une2020_avg[order(-une2020_avg$value), ], 5) } #>     country_name    value #> 1      Australia 6.478989 #> 2 United Kingdom 4.600000 options(op) # }

title: List OECD member countries — list_oecd_countries
text: Returns data frame 38 OECD member countries ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 codes English names. network call required.

title: List OECD member countries — list_oecd_countries
code: list_oecd_countries()

title: List OECD member countries — list_oecd_countries
text: data frame columns: iso3 ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code (character) name English country name (character)

title: List OECD member countries — list_oecd_countries
code: list_oecd_countries() #>    iso3            name #> 1   AUS       Australia #> 2   AUT         Austria #> 3   BEL         Belgium #> 4   CAN          Canada #> 5   CHL           Chile #> 6   COL        Colombia #> 7   CRI      Costa Rica #> 8   CZE  Czech Republic #> 9   DNK         Denmark #> 10  EST         Estonia #> 11  FIN         Finland #> 12  FRA          France #> 13  DEU         Germany #> 14  GRC          Greece #> 15  HUN         Hungary #> 16  ISL         Iceland #> 17  IRL         Ireland #> 18  ISR          Israel #> 19  ITA           Italy #> 20  JPN           Japan #> 21  KOR           Korea #> 22  LVA          Latvia #> 23  LTU       Lithuania #> 24  LUX      Luxembourg #> 25  MEX          Mexico #> 26  NLD     Netherlands #> 27  NZL     New Zealand #> 28  NOR          Norway #> 29  POL          Poland #> 30  PRT        Portugal #> 31  SVK Slovak Republic #> 32  SVN        Slovenia #> 33  ESP           Spain #> 34  SWE          Sweden #> 35  CHE     Switzerland #> 36  TUR         Turkiye #> 37  GBR  United Kingdom #> 38  USA   United States

what: readoecd: Download and Tidy Data from the 'OECD' — readoecd-package
title: readoecd: Download and Tidy Data from the 'OECD' — readoecd-package
text: Provides clean, tidy access key economic indicators published 'Organisation Economic Co-operation Development' ('OECD'), covering GDP, CPI inflation, unemployment, tax revenue, government deficit, health expenditure, education expenditure, income inequality, labour productivity, current account balance across 38 'OECD' member countries. Data downloaded 'OECD Data Explorer' API https://data-explorer.oecd.org first use cached locally subsequent calls. Returns tidy long-format data frames ready analysis visualisation.

title: readoecd: Download and Tidy Data from the 'OECD' — readoecd-package
text: Maintainer: Charles Coverdale charlesfcoverdale@gmail.com

title: readoecd 0.3.4
text: Fixed incorrect function name NEWS.md (get_oecd_trade corrected get_oecd_current_account). Fixed cache tag bug get_oecd_unemployment() requesting countries. Standardised NULL check pattern across data functions.

title: readoecd 0.3.3
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-18 Examples now wrapped try() handle transient OECD API failures gracefully CRAN checks.

title: readoecd 0.3.2
text: Removed non-existent pkgdown URL DESCRIPTION.

title: readoecd 0.3.1
text: Examples now cache tempdir() instead user’s home directory, fixing CRAN policy compliance \donttest examples. Cache directory now configurable via options(readoecd.cache_dir = ...).

title: readoecd 0.3.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-12 Added get_oecd_cpi() — annual CPI inflation rate, year--year (OECD Prices database). Added get_oecd_deficit() — general government net lending/borrowing % GDP (NAAG). Added get_oecd_inequality() — Gini coefficient disposable income (OECD IDD).

title: readoecd 0.2.0
text: Added get_oecd_gdp() — annual GDP current prices (OECD National Accounts). Added get_oecd_tax() — total tax revenue % GDP (OECD Revenue Statistics). Added get_oecd_health() — total health expenditure % GDP (SHA database). Added get_oecd_education() — total education expenditure % GDP (EAG UOE Finance). Added get_oecd_productivity() — GDP per hour worked per capita (OECD PDB). Added get_oecd_current_account() — annual current account balance USD (OECD BOP).

title: readoecd 0.1.0
text: Initial release. get_oecd_unemployment() — monthly unemployment rates 38 OECD members. Utility functions: list_oecd_countries(), check_oecd_api(), clear_oecd_cache(). Data downloaded OECD Data Explorer API first use cached locally.
